  "market_assessment": {

    "reasoning": "Nifty -0.62%, Bank Nifty -1.01%, India VIX +2.59% to 17.05 — broader market in mild risk-off but IT and Pharma showing relative strength. Several portfolio names had explosive Q4 prints today (COFORGE +17%, SRF +10.6%, PIDILITIND +9.3%, POLYCAB +9.4%, BHARATFORG +7.1%, BAJAJ-AUTO +6.5%, CAMS +5.3%) — book is up +1.24% intraday. Priority is locking in gains via SL trails on the post-results pops, not new admits.",

      "nifty_support": 24100,
      "nifty_resistance": 24300
    }
  },

  "watchlist_notes": "Bullish candidates evaluated and rejected for new entry: SRF (already held), POLYCAB (already held), CAMS (already held), PIDILITIND (already held), BAJAJ-AUTO (already held), COFORGE (already held), MARICO (already held), GRSE (already held), WOCKPHARMA (already held), ABCAPITAL (already held), PNBHOUSING (already held), BHARATFORG (already held). Fresh BULLISH names not held: KEI rejected on Rule 13 — Income Tax raids on May 7 at 6 locations is an active regulatory overhang, NO_ACTION mandated regardless of strong Q4. INDUSINDBK rejected on Rule 13 — SFIO/SEBI overhang status not confirmed resolved; consensus target Rs 852 below CMP 950 (16 Sells vs 6 Buys) — pass. NUVAMA, TATATECH, FINCABLES, GABRIEL, THERMAX, RAMCOCEM, APOLLOTYRE, LAURUSLABS, DABUR, PERSISTENT, LUPIN — book is already at 61% deployed across 13 names with extensive Q4 binary winners; cluster discipline (Capital Goods cluster: GRSE+POLYCAB; Financial Services: PNBHOUSING+CAMS+ABCAPITAL; FMCG: MARICO; Chemicals: SRF+PIDILITIND; Pharma: WOCKPHARMA; Auto: BHARATFORG+BAJAJ-AUTO; IT: COFORGE) means most fresh adds would breach single-cluster cap or correlate with existing winners on the same Q4-results theme. DABUR pending LIMIT 487 has been working 357 minutes — should evaluate cancel next tick if not filled by EOD. HFCL pending MIS LIMIT 600x@133.50 has been OPEN 3422 minutes (>2 days) — STALE, should be cancelled.",
  "portfolio_notes": "Book is +1.24% intraday on widespread Q4 beats (COFORGE +17%, SRF +10.6%, PIDILITIND +9.3%, POLYCAB +9.4%). Deployed 61% — within soft target. Today's primary action is SL discipline: trailed MARICO/PIDILITIND/SRF up by small increments while respecting Rule (c) 1% trail-gap floor (avoiding the Day-10 self-stopout pattern). Main risk is afternoon profit-booking on the Q4 winners cluster. Cumulative experiment P&L still -1,859 entering today; this session's unrealized +12,559 is the strongest day so far — DO NOT panic-exit ahead of SLs (Rule 17, Day-12 lesson). Stale HFCL pending order from prior session should be cancelled — flagging for next tick (no cancel decision in current schema slot)."
